Apply when deciding whether to ship with shortcuts, refactor before adding features, or prioritize debt repayment in a sprint. Reckless deliberate debt requires escalation; deliberate prudent debt is acceptable when documented with location, impact per week, and trigger conditions to prevent silent erosion into inadvertent reckless debt.

methodologies.name: workflows
methodologies.description: Architecture workflows provide repeatable processes for system design, reviews, decisions, and migrations. Use for design-first development, technology evaluation, ADR management, and legacy modernization. Delivers standardized workflows that compress decision cycles, reduce review overhead, and improve team alignment on architecture.

domains.description: Software Developer is the orchestrator for development work, routing to specialized sub-skills. Use as entry point for any development task—it identifies whether you need API design, frontend work, architecture decisions, automation setup, or code quality improvements. Integrates knowledge across programming languages, frameworks, and development practices.

methodologies.name: ab-testing
methodologies.description: A/B testing implementation enables controlled experimentation with event tracking, statistical analysis, and result reporting. Apply when validating features, optimizing user experience, or improving conversion rates with sufficient traffic. Delivers statistically significant results with proper experiment assignment, conversion tracking, and power calculations.

methodologies.name: accessibility
methodologies.description: Accessibility ensures applications work for all users including those using assistive technologies. Apply to all public-facing applications as a legal requirement and usability practice. Delivers WCAG 2.1 compliant interfaces with semantic HTML, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, sufficient color contrast, and automated testing.

methodologies.name: api-authentication
methodologies.description: API authentication secures endpoints using API keys, JWT, OAuth 2.0, or mTLS appropriate to use case. Apply to all APIs handling user data or sensitive operations. Delivers secure credential validation, scope-based authorization, token lifecycle management, and protection against common attacks like token revocation bypass.

methodologies.name: api-contract-first
methodologies.description: Contract-first development designs API specifications before implementation, enabling parallel frontend/backend work. Apply when multiple teams integrate or building client SDKs. Delivers generated server stubs, client libraries, tests, and validated implementations that match documented contracts.

methodologies.name: api-documentation
methodologies.description: API documentation tools like Swagger UI, Redoc, or Stoplight make APIs discoverable with working examples. Apply to all public APIs and internal services. Delivers interactive documentation with try-it-out functionality, SDKs in multiple languages, and clear authentication/rate limit guidance.

methodologies.name: api-error-handling
methodologies.description: Error handling using RFC 7807 (Problem Details) provides structured, actionable error responses. Apply to all REST APIs to improve client debugging and compliance. Delivers consistent error format with error codes, field-level validation messages, trace IDs for debugging, and links to documentation.

methodologies.name: api-graphql
methodologies.description: GraphQL enables flexible client queries with schema-defined data graphs, subscriptions, and federation. Apply when clients need different data shapes, enabling field-level authorization and reducing over-fetching. Delivers efficient queries with DataLoader batching, cursor-based pagination, proper error handling, and microservices federation.

methodologies.name: api-openapi-spec
methodologies.description: OpenAPI specification documents REST APIs in machine-readable format enabling code generation and tooling. Apply to all REST APIs for documentation, client generation, and contract validation. Delivers interactive Swagger UI, generated clients in multiple languages, validated server stubs, and automated documentation.

methodologies.name: api-rate-limiting
methodologies.description: Rate limiting protects APIs from abuse and ensures fair resource usage with algorithms like token bucket or sliding window. Apply to all public APIs and authenticated endpoints to prevent DoS and manage capacity. Delivers configurable per-user/tier/endpoint limits with clear Retry-After headers and tiered service tiers.

methodologies.name: api-rest-design
methodologies.description: REST API design provides resource-oriented endpoints using standard HTTP methods and status codes. Apply to all REST services for consistency and client compatibility. Delivers intuitive URLs, correct status codes, HATEOAS links for discoverability, and pagination supporting filtering and sorting.

methodologies.name: api-versioning
methodologies.description: API versioning maintains backward compatibility while evolving endpoints using URL paths, headers, or content negotiation. Apply when making breaking changes to live APIs. Delivers multiple supported versions simultaneously with deprecation warnings, sunset headers, and migration guides for clients.

methodologies.name: browser-automation
methodologies.description: Browser automation using Puppeteer/Playwright enables web scraping, E2E testing, screenshots, PDFs, and form automation. Apply for testing web apps, generating reports from web content, or automation tasks requiring browser rendering. Delivers reliable cross-browser scripts with proper error handling, retry logic, and performance optimization.

methodologies.name: caching-strategy
methodologies.description: Multi-level caching reduces latency, database load, and infrastructure costs through cache-aside, write-through, and write-behind patterns. Apply when reads heavily outweigh writes or expensive computations repeat. Delivers reduced response times with TTL management, invalidation strategies, HTTP header control, and proper failure handling.

methodologies.name: contract-first-development
methodologies.description: Contract-first development designs API specifications before implementation using OpenAPI, enabling client/server teams to work independently. Apply in service-oriented architectures where clients integrate with servers. Delivers generated stubs and SDKs, automatic validation against spec in CI/CD, and specification-driven development.

methodologies.name: database-design
methodologies.description: Database design structures data for integrity, performance, and scalability through normalization, indexing, and partitioning. Apply when starting projects with persistent storage or optimizing slow queries. Delivers ACID-compliant schemas, optimized indexes, clear relationships, and strategies for growth like partitioning and denormalization.

methodologies.name: design-tokens
methodologies.description: Design tokens store visual design decisions as atomic values ensuring consistency across platforms and enabling theming. Apply to design systems, multi-platform apps, and white-label solutions. Delivers single source of truth for colors, spacing, typography enabling theme switching and design-to-code handoff.

methodologies.name: django-celery
methodologies.description: Django Celery enables asynchronous task processing with queues, retries, scheduling, and idempotency. Apply for long-running operations, batch processing, scheduled jobs, and decoupling request handling from processing. Delivers reliable background job execution with automatic retries, exponential backoff, timeouts, and monitoring.

methodologies.name: django-services
methodologies.description: Django services architecture separates business logic from views and models into reusable service functions. Apply to all Django projects to reduce view bloat and improve testability. Delivers focused business logic in dedicated service modules, proper dependency injection, and clear separation of concerns.

methodologies.name: feature-flags
methodologies.description: Feature flags enable controlled feature rollout, A/B testing, and quick killswitches without redeployment. Apply for production releases, testing with subsets of users, or gradual migrations. Delivers fine-grained feature control with per-user targeting, percentage rollouts, and real-time toggles for rapid response to issues.

methodologies.name: frontend-design
methodologies.description: Addresses the challenge of designing modern UI systems through a structured workflow combining requirements gathering, multi-variant generation, user selection, component library setup, and story-driven development. Delivers polished design systems quickly by automating brainstorming iterations and maintaining design consistency across teams through Storybook documentation and component reusability.

methodologies.name: go-concurrency-patterns
methodologies.description: Solves concurrent operation management in Go through proven patterns like worker pools and fan-out/fan-in architectures. Enables safe parallel processing using goroutines, channels, and synchronization primitives to maximize throughput while preventing race conditions and resource exhaustion.

methodologies.name: go-error-handling-patterns
methodologies.description: Tackles explicit error handling unique to Go by treating errors as first-class values rather than exceptions. Provides techniques for wrapping errors with context, checking sentinel values, creating custom error types, and implementing middleware for consistent error propagation throughout the application.

methodologies.name: go-project-structure
methodologies.description: Organizes Go applications using community conventions with cmd/ for binaries, internal/ for private code, and pkg/ for public libraries. Delivers maintainability and team collaboration by establishing clear package hierarchies, dependency management with go.mod, and separation between application entry points and business logic.

methodologies.name: go-standard-layout
methodologies.description: Establishes consistent Go project organization following the standard layout with internal packages, multiple entry points, and clear layered architecture. Enables scalability and maintainability by preventing external code from accessing implementation details while making the project immediately familiar to Go developers.

methodologies.name: graphql-api
methodologies.description: Solves over-fetching and under-fetching problems endemic to REST by providing clients flexible querying with exact data selection. Delivers efficient data fetching for complex UIs, real-time capabilities through subscriptions, and prevention of N+1 queries using DataLoader batching strategies.

methodologies.name: graphql-api-design
methodologies.description: Addresses API versioning and backward compatibility challenges by designing schemas that evolve without versioning through strong typing and introspection. Enables rapid frontend development by letting clients request only needed fields, providing powerful developer tools, and supporting complex data relationships with built-in subscription support.

methodologies.name: internationalization
methodologies.description: Handles multiple language support, locale-specific formatting, and cultural adaptation without hardcoding strings. Delivers truly global applications by managing translations, plural forms, date/number formatting, RTL language support, and currency conversion while maintaining a single codebase across regions.

methodologies.name: logging-patterns
methodologies.description: Provides visibility into application behavior through structured logging with machine-parseable formats, request correlation, and appropriate level stratification. Delivers debugging efficiency and operational awareness by capturing meaningful context without exposing sensitive data, enabling rapid incident response and performance analysis.

methodologies.name: message-queues
methodologies.description: Decouples distributed services and handles asynchronous workloads through reliable message passing patterns including work queues, pub/sub, and event sourcing. Delivers resilience, load leveling, and scalability by enabling systems to gracefully handle failures, retries, dead letter queues, and bursty traffic.

methodologies.name: monorepo-turborepo
methodologies.description: Manages multiple packages and applications within a single repository using intelligent caching and incremental builds. Delivers faster CI/CD, code reuse, atomic commits, and team consistency by enabling parallel task execution and never rebuilding unchanged code across the organization.

methodologies.name: nextjs-app-router
methodologies.description: Modernizes Next.js development using server components, file-based routing, and collocated layouts by default. Delivers reduced JavaScript shipped to clients, simplified data fetching, and composable page architecture by making server-side rendering the default with client-side interactivity only where needed.

methodologies.name: nodejs-service-layer
methodologies.description: Separates business logic from HTTP handling using the Controller-Service-Repository pattern with dependency injection. Delivers testable, maintainable code by creating thin controllers that only handle requests, fat services containing logic, and repositories abstracting data access from business concerns.

methodologies.name: nosql-patterns
methodologies.description: Addresses data modeling for flexible, horizontally-scalable systems by designing around access patterns rather than entities. Delivers high-volume data handling and specialized query optimization by choosing the right NoSQL type (document, key-value, wide-column, or graph) and embracing denormalization for specific use cases.

methodologies.name: openapi-specification
methodologies.description: Solves documentation drift and manual client generation through a machine-readable API contract. Delivers single source of truth for APIs enabling automatic SDK generation, server stub creation, interactive documentation, and validation across all client languages and teams.

methodologies.name: performance-testing
methodologies.description: Identifies bottlenecks and validates performance requirements through load testing, profiling, and benchmarking before deployment. Delivers confidence in system scalability by measuring baseline performance, detecting regressions, and optimizing critical paths using realistic production-like scenarios and instrumentation.

methodologies.name: pwa-development
methodologies.description: Bridges web and native app experiences through service workers, offline capability, and installable manifests. Delivers app-like user experiences without app store dependency by enabling offline functionality, push notifications, background sync, and home screen installation across all platforms with web technologies.

methodologies.name: python-async-patterns
methodologies.description: Handles I/O-bound operations efficiently without blocking the event loop using asyncio, coroutines, and task coordination. Delivers high-concurrency APIs and background job processing by enabling thousands of concurrent operations with minimal resource overhead through proper async/await patterns and structured concurrency.

methodologies.name: rate-limiting
methodologies.description: Protects APIs from abuse and overload through request quotas and fair resource allocation across users. Delivers service stability and fair usage by implementing sliding window algorithms, tiered limits, distributed rate limiting via Redis, and clear feedback through standard HTTP headers.

methodologies.name: react-component-architecture
methodologies.description: Organizes React components for scalability and reusability through feature-based directory structure and composition patterns. Delivers maintainable UIs and efficient development by establishing clear component responsibilities, avoiding prop drilling, using custom hooks for logic extraction, and building design systems incrementally.

methodologies.name: rest-api-design
methodologies.description: Establishes consistent, intuitive API interfaces through resource-oriented design using HTTP methods and status codes correctly. Delivers developer-friendly APIs by following predictable patterns, proper status codes, pagination, filtering, and HATEOAS links enabling clients to discover relationships without documentation memorization.

methodologies.name: rust-tokio-async
methodologies.description: Manages concurrent operations in Rust using Tokio's async/await runtime and structured concurrency patterns. Delivers high-performance concurrent services by enabling thousands of concurrent I/O operations through lightweight async tasks without thread overhead, with proper cancellation and cleanup semantics.

methodologies.name: security-testing
methodologies.description: Security testing identifies vulnerabilities before production by combining static analysis, dynamic testing, dependency scanning, and penetration testing. Apply this methodology when handling sensitive data, before major releases, or after significant code changes. The practical value is early vulnerability detection that prevents costly production incidents and compliance violations.

methodologies.name: seo-for-spas
methodologies.description: SEO for Single Page Applications addresses the challenge of making JavaScript-heavy sites indexable by search engines through server-side rendering, dynamic meta tags, and structured data. Use this when building public-facing SPAs, content-heavy sites, or e-commerce platforms where search visibility is critical. It delivers organic traffic without relying solely on paid marketing.

methodologies.name: shadcn-ui-architecture
methodologies.description: shadcn/ui provides copy-based component primitives built with Tailwind and Radix, enabling rapid UI development without npm package complexity. Apply when building React applications that need consistent, accessible components quickly. The value is shipping faster with copy-paste components while maintaining full customization control.

methodologies.name: sql-optimization
methodologies.description: SQL optimization improves database query performance through index design, query rewriting, and execution plan analysis using EXPLAIN. Use when facing slow database queries, high CPU usage, or application performance degradation under load. The practical value is often 10-100x speedup for problematic queries without architectural changes.

methodologies.name: tailwind-architecture
methodologies.description: Tailwind CSS is a utility-first framework that composes designs from atomic classes, with configuration-driven customization and design tokens. Apply when building responsive UIs rapidly with consistent design systems. It delivers faster development and smaller CSS payloads through JIT compilation and automatic purging.

methodologies.name: ui-component-library
methodologies.description: A UI component library provides reusable, accessible interface elements with consistent APIs, reducing code duplication and ensuring design consistency. Apply when building multiple products, collaborating with larger teams, or scaling design systems. The practical value is faster feature development, fewer bugs, and easier onboarding for new team members.

methodologies.name: websocket-design
methodologies.description: WebSocket design implements bidirectional real-time communication with connection lifecycle management, heartbeats, and scaling strategies for multi-server deployments. Use when building real-time features like chat, notifications, or live data streams. It eliminates polling overhead and enables responsive server-to-client communication at scale.

methodologies.name: xp-extreme-programming
methodologies.description: Extreme Programming emphasizes technical excellence through pair programming, test-driven development, continuous integration, and sustainable pace. Apply to small-to-medium teams with available customers and code quality requirements. It delivers high confidence in changes, reduces bugs reaching production, and maintains team morale through sustainable work.

domains.description: Testing Developer covers testing methodologies including unit testing, integration testing, e2e testing, performance testing, contract testing, mutation testing, and test automation. Use when designing test strategies, writing test suites, ensuring code coverage, or validating system behavior. Focuses on testing frameworks, test data strategy, and continuous testing practices.

methodologies.name: security-testing
methodologies.description: Security testing validates application defenses against OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities through automated scanning, manual testing, and dependency analysis. Apply before production deployments, when introducing new features with authentication or data handling, or when integrating third-party libraries. It provides systematic vulnerability detection that shifts security testing left into development.

domains.description: CI/CD pipeline engineering for automated build, test, and deployment workflows. This domain covers GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, and other automation platforms, helping teams move code from development to production reliably and at scale. Use this when you need to design build pipelines, set up automated testing, implement deployment automation, create rollback strategies, or manage multi-environment deployments across infrastructure.

methodologies.name: docker-compose
methodologies.description: Docker Compose orchestrates multi-container applications with declarative YAML configuration for services, networks, and volumes. Use for local development environments, integration testing, and single-host deployments where Kubernetes is overkill. It simplifies spinning up complete environments and makes infrastructure portable across machines.

domains.description: DevOps practices bridging development and operations with infrastructure automation and deployment excellence. Covers containerization, orchestration, infrastructure-as-code, monitoring, logging, and operational tooling to enable fast, reliable releases. Use this when building deployment pipelines, managing cloud infrastructure, setting up monitoring and alerting, handling production incidents, or scaling systems for reliability.

methodologies.name: docker-compose
methodologies.description: Docker Compose defines and runs multi-container applications with built-in dependency management, health checks, and resource limits. Apply when setting up staging environments, testing microservices locally, or deploying to single servers. The value is reduced operational complexity and faster environment provisioning for non-Kubernetes deployments.

domains.description: Infrastructure architecture and cloud platform engineering for scalable, resilient systems. Covers AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes, Terraform, networking, security, and capacity planning. Use this when designing cloud infrastructure, selecting services and tools, planning disaster recovery, managing multi-region deployments, optimizing cloud costs, or building self-healing infrastructure.

methodologies.name: docker-compose
methodologies.description: Docker Compose provides multi-container orchestration with service composition, networking isolation, and volume persistence for applications spanning multiple containers. Use for single-host deployments, testing multi-service architectures, or developing with real database dependencies. It bridges local development and production infrastructure patterns without Kubernetes overhead.

domains.description: Server configuration, hardening, tuning, and developer experience for Linux VPS and dedicated servers. Covers SSH hardening, firewall management, kernel tuning, nginx reverse proxy, SSL/TLS, systemd services, Docker Compose, backup automation, monitoring, deploy scripts, and AI agent development tuning. Use this for single-server setups, multi-project hosting, hardening for production, shell productivity workflows, and bootstrapping new servers from scratch.

methodologies.name: agent-dev-tuning
methodologies.description: Agent development tuning optimizes VPS servers for Claude Code and AI agent workflows through inotify limits, memory management, file descriptor tuning, and worktree configuration. Apply when running multiple agent instances, developing on large codebases, or using file watchers extensively. It prevents resource exhaustion and enables parallel agent execution with proper isolation.

methodologies.name: backup-recovery
methodologies.description: Backup strategy and disaster recovery implementation covers database dumps, configuration snapshots, encrypted offsite backup with restic, and recovery runbooks. Apply to any VPS hosting critical services where data loss would be catastrophic. It ensures your entire platform can be reconstructed within hours from offsite encrypted storage.

methodologies.name: bash-aliases
methodologies.description: Bash aliases organize command shortcuts by category, from simple substitutions to function-based wrappers with parameter support and completion awareness. Use to reduce repetitive typing, prevent mistakes with safety aliases, and document common operations. The value is measurable time savings and fewer errors in daily server operations.

methodologies.name: cron-automation
methodologies.description: Cron scheduling with locking, environment handling, and logging enables autonomous task execution from backups to monitoring without external tooling. Apply to schedule daily backups, hourly health checks, or regular maintenance tasks. It provides reliable background job execution with proper error handling and prevents overlapping runs.

methodologies.name: deploy-scripts
methodologies.description: Deploy scripts automate code deployment from workspace to runtime with dependency resolution, virtualenv management, and service restart orchestration. Use for any Python/Node application where manual deployment is error-prone. It ensures consistent, reproducible deployments with rollback capability and post-deploy validation.

methodologies.name: direnv-mise-versions
methodologies.description: direnv and mise manage per-project environments by loading .env files and setting Ruby/Python versions automatically on directory entry. Apply when developing multiple projects with different version requirements. The value is eliminating setup errors, preventing version conflicts, and automating environment activation.

methodologies.name: docker-compose-patterns
methodologies.description: Docker Compose patterns cover production-grade multi-container orchestration including health checks, network isolation, resource limits, and rollout strategies. Use when deploying containerized applications to single-host servers with high availability requirements. It provides safer deployments with health-driven dependencies and resource guardrails.

methodologies.name: dotfiles-management
methodologies.description: Dotfiles management tracks shell configuration, editor settings, and system preferences in version control for reproducible environments across machines. Apply to keep development environments synchronized and documented. The value is faster machine setup, consistency across team members, and portable workflows.

methodologies.name: fail2ban-setup
methodologies.description: Fail2ban protects SSH and web services from brute force attacks by automatically blocking offending IPs based on configurable rules and ban durations. Use on any publicly accessible server to reduce intrusion attempts and brute force load. It provides a simple, effective layer of protection without complex WAF infrastructure.

methodologies.name: firewall-management
methodologies.description: UFW firewall rules control network traffic with simple allow/deny policies, stateful connections, and port management across services. Apply to restrict access to sensitive services and reduce attack surface. It prevents unauthorized access while maintaining necessary service connectivity with minimal configuration overhead.

methodologies.name: git-server-workflow
methodologies.description: Separates workspace (development) from runtime (production) for solo developer platforms, using rsync-based deployment and git-based rollback strategies. This eliminates the friction of managing code and services in a single directory, allowing agents to develop safely while services run independently. The pattern solves deployment reliability for single-server setups without CI/CD complexity.

methodologies.name: health-checks-autoheal
methodologies.description: Implements HTTP health endpoints, systemd watchdog integration, and automated service restart on failure for multi-service VPS platforms. Covers circuit breaker patterns and Celery worker inspection to catch failures before users notice them. Essential for platforms where manual recovery would interrupt work.

methodologies.name: kernel-tuning
methodologies.description: Configures kernel parameters via sysctl for AI agent workloads, focusing on TCP performance (BBR), inotify watch limits (critical for file watchers), memory management, and security hardening. Default Ubuntu settings are conservative; this methodology tunes for high-concurrency web services and developer tools like Claude Code.

methodologies.name: monitoring-logging
methodologies.description: Provides lightweight monitoring without enterprise stacks: journalctl patterns for log filtering, tmux-based status dashboards, health check scripts, and FLOW-style autonomous monitoring reports. Designed for solo developers who need 95% of Prometheus value with 5% of the complexity.

methodologies.name: multi-project-hosting
methodologies.description: Covers port allocation conventions, nginx multi-domain configuration, Cloudflare DNS integration, and resource planning for 2-5 projects on a single 30GB VPS. Solves the architectural question of shared vs isolated services and when to split to a second server.

methodologies.name: nginx-reverse-proxy
methodologies.description: Configures nginx as the single entry point for web traffic, handling SSL termination, WebSocket upgrades, security headers, rate limiting, and Cloudflare integration. The snippets pattern enables DRY configuration across multiple domains.

methodologies.name: secrets-management
methodologies.description: Manages environment variables, .env file patterns, 1Password CLI integration for programmatic access, and secret rotation strategies. Prevents hardcoded credentials in code, docker images, and logs while supporting server-side automation.

methodologies.name: server-init-bootstrap
methodologies.description: Complete first-login checklist for Ubuntu 24.04 VPS: user creation, SSH hardening, hostname/timezone setup, essential packages, firewall (UFW), fail2ban, unattended-upgrades, and systemd user service initialization. Takes a fresh server from root access to production-ready.

methodologies.name: shell-productivity
methodologies.description: Replaces traditional Unix utilities with modern alternatives (eza, bat, fd, ripgrep, fzf) that add syntax highlighting, git integration, fuzzy search, and smart defaults. Saves developers significant time daily when managing multiple projects over SSH.

methodologies.name: ssh-hardening
methodologies.description: Implements key-only authentication with ed25519 keys, cryptographic hardening (restricting to modern algorithms), access control (AllowUsers, MaxAuthTries), and optional port changes. Eliminates brute-force password attacks and prepares SSH infrastructure for DevOps.

methodologies.name: ssl-tls-management
methodologies.description: Covers certificate lifecycle with Let's Encrypt (certbot for auto-renewal), Cloudflare origin certificates (15-year validity), HSTS headers, OCSP stapling, and nginx SSL hardening. Simplifies HTTPS setup for servers behind Cloudflare or standalone.

methodologies.name: swap-memory-management
methodologies.description: Configures swap files, tunes vm.swappiness and memory parameters, sets OOM killer scores per service, and establishes per-service memory limits via systemd cgroups. Prevents cascading failures when one service consumes too much RAM.

methodologies.name: systemd-user-services
methodologies.description: Creates application services that run under a regular user (not root) with resource limits, environment file injection, automatic restart on failure, and journald logging. Replaces manual process management with reliable service lifecycle control.

methodologies.name: tmux-power-user
methodologies.description: Configures tmux for persistent remote sessions with plugins (resurrect, continuum for auto-save), pane management, custom status bars showing system metrics, and session templates for project contexts. Survives SSH disconnections and powers multi-pane development workflows.

methodologies.name: unattended-upgrades
methodologies.description: Automates security patching via systemd timers, configures auto-reboot scheduling for off-hours, blacklists packages requiring manual migration (databases), and cleans up old kernels. Keeps servers patched without manual intervention while maintaining stability.

methodologies.name: wireguard-vpn
methodologies.description: Sets up modern VPN using cryptokey routing with minimal configuration, supports site-to-site connections between VPS and home networks, and handles full-tunnel and split-tunnel modes. Simpler and faster than OpenVPN for solo developers connecting multiple locations.

domains.description: Content strategy, copywriting, SEO optimization, email marketing, social media, and AI-powered content creation across channels. Covers editorial planning, keyword targeting, long-form content, email sequences, video and podcast production, and tools for scaled content distribution. Use this when building organic traffic through content, growing email lists, launching social media presence, producing video or podcast, or implementing AI content workflows.

methodologies.name: growth-content-marketing
methodologies.description: Structures content marketing around a flywheel: research audience problems, create pillar-based content, distribute across owned and earned channels, and measure ROI. Moves beyond random blog posts to strategic content that compounds in SEO value over 6-12 months.

methodologies.name: growth-copywriting-fundamentals
methodologies.description: Teaches the Know-Hook-Hold-Convince-Convert framework for writing that sells. Covers headline formulas, benefit-focused copy, objection handling, and calls to action. Practical frameworks make copywriting a learnable skill rather than mystical talent.

methodologies.name: growth-customer-testimonials
methodologies.description: Systematically collects, improves, and displays social proof to reduce purchase friction. Covers the hierarchy of trust (video > named testimonials > anonymous reviews), asking better questions, and optimal placement on landing pages and pricing.

methodologies.name: growth-email-marketing
methodologies.description: Covers list building with lead magnets, welcome sequences that hook new subscribers, newsletter strategy (VALUE framework), and sales sequences for launches. Email remains the only channel you fully own, making it critical for audience retention.

methodologies.name: growth-newsletter-growth
methodologies.description: Systematizes newsletter growth through lead magnets, signup form optimization, traffic sources (content, Twitter, SEO, guest posts), and referral mechanics. Turns newsletter from hope into predictable growth channel.

methodologies.name: growth-onboarding-emails
methodologies.description: Designs behavior-triggered email sequences that guide new users to activation, with escalation paths for stuck users and personalization by segment. Turns generic welcome emails into conversion machines.

methodologies.name: growth-podcast-strategy
methodologies.description: Two approaches: being a guest on others' podcasts (faster ROI, no equipment needed) or hosting your own (long-term asset). Covers finding shows, pitching guests, interview prep, and measuring downloads and leads.

methodologies.name: growth-reddit-marketing
methodologies.description: Builds reputation in niche subreddits over 4-8 weeks of genuine participation before any promotion, following the 10:1 rule (value before self-promo). Reddit's hostile-to-marketing culture requires authenticity; done right, a single post can drive thousands of organic visitors.

methodologies.name: growth-tiktok-basics
methodologies.description: Sets up business accounts optimized for 1-3 second hooks that capture attention before viewers scroll, uses TikTok's algorithm (not traditional follower counts) for amplification, and repurposes trending sounds and formats. TikTok rewards authenticity and creativity over production value.

methodologies.name: growth-tiktok-strategies
methodologies.description: Master TikTok's algorithm by focusing on hook mastery in the first 1-3 seconds, consistent daily posting, and strategic trend adaptation. TikTok rewards watch time and completion rate heavily; use curiosity hooks, visual movement, and strong opens to capture attention. Convert viewers to customers through pinned offers, link-in-bio optimization, and community engagement.

methodologies.name: growth-webinar-delivery
methodologies.description: Transform webinars from boring presentations into conversion machines by combining engaging delivery with strategic follow-up. Use polling, interactive exercises, and story-based presentation to keep viewers engaged; structure offers clearly with deadlines and risk reversal. Repurpose content into 10+ assets (blog posts, clips, podcasts) to maximize return on effort.

methodologies.name: growth-webinar-planning
methodologies.description: Generate leads through webinars by planning 45-60 minute sessions that solve a pressing problem and build authority with your audience. Build anticipation 3+ weeks before launch through email, social, and partner cross-promotion; design content to educate first (80%) then pitch (20%) to maintain credibility and attendee trust.

methodologies.name: growth-youtube-strategy
methodologies.description: YouTube is the second-largest search engine and best for trust-building; start with your unique niche, consistent publishing schedule, and valuable content regardless of production quality. Optimize titles with keywords and benefits, create thumbnails with emotional expression, and structure videos with strong hooks—consistency beats perfection for growing a subscriber base.

methodologies.name: search-everywhere-optimization
methodologies.description: Modern search is fragmented across Google, ChatGPT, Perplexity, TikTok, and Amazon. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) means becoming the source AI models cite; focus on topic authority, clear structured content, and schema markup for AI readability. AEO optimizes for zero-click results and featured snippets with concise answers, proper headings, and Q&A formats.

domains.description: Conversion rate optimization through data-driven testing, user experience improvements, and behavioral psychology. Covers A/B testing, funnel analysis, form optimization, landing page design, checkout flows, and behavioral segmentation. Use this when you need to improve conversion metrics, reduce friction in user flows, increase revenue from existing traffic, or validate hypotheses through experimentation.

methodologies.name: growth-landing-page-design
methodologies.description: Your landing page is where traffic converts to customers; follow AIDA (Attention→Interest→Desire→Action) with a clear value proposition, social proof, and single primary CTA. Remove friction, lead with benefits over features, and address objections through FAQ and testimonials; test variations and aim for 5-10% conversion rates.

domains.description: Growth marketing and scaling through rapid experimentation, product-led growth, and data-driven tactics. Covers growth loops, viral mechanics, referral programs, retention optimization, analytics, and channel strategy. Use this when you need sustained growth beyond launch, when building network effects, optimizing unit economics, or balancing acquisition with retention.

methodologies.name: ops-automation-workflow
methodologies.description: Stop doing repetitive tasks and build systems that work for you; automation ROI = (time saved per occurrence × occurrences per month) / (time to build + monthly maintenance). Start with lead capture, onboarding sequences, and reporting; use Zapier, Make, or n8n to connect tools without code.

methodologies.name: ops-dashboard-setup
methodologies.description: Centralize scattered metrics into a single glanceable dashboard with 5-10 key KPIs that matter for your business. Design for your north star metric (MRR, MAU, etc.), update daily or weekly, and ensure every number is actionable—avoid vanity metrics that don't drive decisions.

methodologies.name: plausible-analytics
methodologies.description: Plausible is a privacy-first, GDPR-compliant analytics alternative to Google Analytics that doesn't require consent. Track custom events (signups, purchases), goals, and revenue; use the API for dashboards and reports; self-host via Nginx proxy for added privacy and brand control.

domains.description: Go-to-market strategy for product launches and market entry. Covers positioning, messaging, channel selection, pricing strategy, launch sequencing, and competitive positioning. Use this when planning a product launch, entering a new market segment, repositioning an existing product, or coordinating a multi-channel market entry.

methodologies.name: growth-app-store-optimization
methodologies.description: App stores are crowded; ASO is SEO for apps and requires optimizing metadata (name, subtitle, keywords) and visuals (icon, screenshots, video) for both visibility and conversion. Research keywords using competitor analysis and store autocomplete; handle reviews proactively by responding within 24 hours and requesting upgrades after positive experiences.

methodologies.name: growth-cold-outreach
methodologies.description: Cold outreach scales faster than content and cheaper than ads but requires relevance, value, and human touch to avoid spam. Build highly targeted prospect lists (100-200 to start), research each person for personalization, write concise 3-5 sentence emails with low-friction CTAs, and follow up 3-4 times over 21 days.

methodologies.name: growth-hacker-news-launch
methodologies.description: Hacker News rewards substance, authenticity, and technical depth while penalizing marketing speak. Build karma through 2-4 weeks of genuine participation before posting; use 'Show HN' format for products; optimize your landing page for fast load and clear demo; respond thoughtfully to all feedback without defensiveness.

methodologies.name: growth-indiehackers-strategy
methodologies.description: IndieHackers is a community for builders, not a billboard; give first (answer questions, help others) for 2-4 weeks before promoting. Build in public transparently—sharing wins AND losses; ask for specific feedback; celebrate milestones; engage with every comment; the makers who win are relationship-builders, not broadcasters.

methodologies.name: growth-product-hunt-launch
methodologies.description: Product Hunt launches can bring thousands of engaged users in one day, but require 4-8 weeks of preparation to build an audience and hunter network. Execute a coordinated launch day with support emails, social posts, and all-day engagement; perfect your listing with compelling tagline, gallery, and first comment explaining the backstory.

methodologies.name: ops-customer-support
methodologies.description: Great support scales through self-service (FAQ, knowledge base) first, then async email, then live chat for paying users—this hierarchy keeps your workload manageable. Turn support tickets into product insights: same question asked 5+ times becomes an FAQ entry; bugs or feature requests that repeat go on the roadmap.

methodologies.name: ops-financial-planning
methodologies.description: Plan cash flow by projecting 3-12 months ahead: starting cash + revenue - expenses = ending cash. Maintain 3+ months runway; reinvest 30-50% of profits into growth (retention, conversion, acquisition); pay yourself consistently; reserve 20% as emergency fund. Review monthly and adjust quarterly.

methodologies.name: ops-pricing-strategy
methodologies.description: Price based on value delivered (10-20% of customer value), not your costs or competitors. Research willingness-to-pay through surveys and competitor analysis; structure 3 tiers (Good/Better/Best); use psychological pricing (charm pricing $49 vs $50); test with A/B testing and raise prices when conversion > 5% or customers volunteer it's cheap.

methodologies.name: ops-subscription-models
methodologies.description: Subscriptions create predictable revenue and ongoing customer relationships; choose your model (SaaS, membership, replenishment, curation, access) and billing frequency (monthly/annual). Design 3-4 feature tiers with clear upgrade paths; measure MRR, churn, LTV, and LTV:CAC ratio (>3:1); automate payment recovery with dunning emails.

domains.description: Search engine optimization covering technical SEO, on-page optimization, link building, and modern AI search adaptation. Covers keyword research, site audits, Core Web Vitals, schema markup, topical authority, Google AI Overviews, and zero-click search optimization. Use this when targeting organic search visibility, improving technical site foundation, building topical authority, optimizing for Google's AI features, or competing for high-value keywords.

methodologies.name: growth-seo-fundamentals
methodologies.description: SEO has 3 pillars: technical (can Google crawl?), on-page (does content match intent?), off-page (do others trust/link to you?). Start with technical audits (Core Web Vitals, mobile-friendly, indexation), optimize content to answer search intent comprehensively, and earn links through valuable assets and relationships—SEO takes 6-12 months but provides free, sustainable traffic.

methodologies.name: growth-seo-link-building
methodologies.description: Create link-worthy content (original research, tools, guides) and outreach to sites that would benefit from linking to it; use competitor analysis, resource pages, and HARO to find opportunities. Personalize outreach (first line matters most), expect 5-20% response rates, follow up once after 7 days, and track everything in a spreadsheet.

methodologies.name: seo
methodologies.description: SEO is the practice of optimizing your website to rank higher in search results for relevant keywords. It encompasses technical setup (robots.txt, XML sitemap, Core Web Vitals), on-page optimization (content, headings, schema), and off-page authority (backlinks, brand mentions). SEO provides sustainable free traffic once you rank.

methodologies.name: seo-basics
methodologies.description: SEO fundamentals include technical setup (robots.txt, sitemap, HTTPS, mobile-friendly, fast load), semantic HTML (proper heading hierarchy, ARIA), multilingual hreflang tags, Core Web Vitals targets (LCP <2.5s, INP <200ms, CLS <0.1), and accessibility standards (WCAG 2.2). These foundations enable crawling, indexing, and AI search visibility.

methodologies.name: seo-techniques
methodologies.description: Advanced SEO tactics include JSON-LD schema markup (Article, Organization, FAQ, Breadcrumb), Open Graph and Twitter Cards for social sharing, internal linking strategy (pillar-cluster model), AI optimization (llms.txt, entity relationships), and breadcrumb navigation. These techniques improve both traditional search visibility and AI assistant citations.

methodologies.name: topical-authority
methodologies.description: Build topical authority by covering entire topics comprehensively rather than targeting keywords individually. Create a pillar page (overview) and supporting cluster pages (subtopics); interlink them; answer all related questions; update regularly; earn backlinks; and build brand mentions. Search engines reward comprehensive topical coverage over scattered keyword targeting.

methodologies.name: zero-click-search-adaptation
methodologies.description: Addresses the shift from click-based to AI-driven search by teaching you how to position content as a cited source in AI overviews and generative engine results. With 60% of searches now resulting in zero clicks, this methodology helps you maintain visibility and traffic by optimizing for citation in AI responses, building brand authority through original research, and adapting metrics beyond traditional CTR.

domains.description: Social media marketing and community management across platforms. Covers platform-specific strategies, content calendars, engagement metrics, influencer collaboration, and paid social advertising. Use this when building social presence, engaging communities, launching viral content, managing multiple platforms simultaneously, or analyzing social performance.

methodologies.name: growth-community-building
methodologies.description: Teaches the framework for building engaged communities that become moats for your business, moving from seed through nurture and scale. You learn to select the right platform, define community value through access and connection, create rituals that drive participation, and measure health through DAU/MAU and retention metrics. Communities generate content, reduce acquisition costs, and provide valuable feedback loops.

methodologies.name: growth-linkedin-strategy
methodologies.description: Provides a complete system for building authority on LinkedIn through profile optimization, strategic content creation, and engagement. The framework covers positioning yourself as a thought leader, posting formats that drive engagement (stories, carousels, lessons), commenting strategies to build visibility, and converting followers into leads through DMs and CTAs. Emphasizes personal storytelling and authentic value-add over corporate speak.

methodologies.name: growth-social-media-strategy
methodologies.description: Establishes a foundation for consistent social media growth using the PACE framework: platform selection, audience definition, content creation, and engagement. You learn to master one platform first, define 3-5 content pillars, maintain consistent posting frequency, and spend daily time engaging with your community. Includes practical templates and metrics to track what's working.

methodologies.name: growth-twitter-x-growth
methodologies.description: Covers the full cycle of growing an audience on Twitter/X from positioning to monetization, focusing on consistency and personality. You define your unique angle, create valuable content across multiple formats (tweets, threads, screenshots), engage heavily with larger accounts to gain visibility, and convert followers into customers through subtle promotion and community building. Emphasizes the importance of replying to 20+ tweets daily.

methodologies.name: instagram-growth
methodologies.description: Details a systematic approach to Instagram growth through daily Reels, Stories, and engagement. The methodology covers content calendars, comment strategies for visibility, and funnels from Reels discovery through DM conversion. You learn to prioritize Reels for reach, use Stories for relationship building, and set up automation to qualify leads before sending links. Includes practical daily engagement routines and metrics.

methodologies.name: threads-growth
methodologies.description: Teaches growth on Threads by leveraging cross-platform synergy with Instagram while building genuine conversations. You master reply strategies that add value, ask questions that encourage discussion, and use Threads for deeper connection building than the character-limited nature of other platforms. Includes templates for conversation starters and practical metrics to track weekly progress.

domains.description: Agile project management using Scrum, Kanban, and iterative frameworks for software development teams. Covers sprint planning, backlog grooming, velocity tracking, retrospectives, and continuous improvement. Use this when managing engineering teams with iterative delivery, when you need transparent progress tracking, or when coordinating work across distributed teams.

methodologies.name: linear-issue-tracking
methodologies.description: Covers the high-velocity workflow of Linear, designed for engineering teams that value speed and keyboard shortcuts. You learn to structure workspaces by team, configure cycles as time-boxed sprints, use projects for initiatives, and leverage triage for inbox management. Includes GitHub integration for automatic status updates and emphasis on opinionated workflows over customization.

methodologies.name: notion-pm
methodologies.description: Teaches how to build flexible PM systems in Notion using databases, relations, rollups, and formulas. You learn to design Projects and Tasks databases with bidirectional relations, create views (board, table, timeline, calendar) for different needs, set up automations with Zapier, and use formulas for progress tracking. Ideal for teams wanting documentation alongside task management.

methodologies.name: trello-kanban
methodologies.description: Provides a complete Trello setup guide covering board structure, card anatomy, labels, Power-Ups, and Butler automation. You learn to set up kanban columns with WIP limits, use labels for consistent categorization, leverage Custom Fields for story points and sprints, and automate workflows with Butler rules and buttons. Emphasizes simplicity and visual management.

domains.description: Project management covering planning, scheduling, resource coordination, risk management, and stakeholder communication. Covers traditional waterfall, hybrid, and adaptive project approaches, budget tracking, milestone management, and team coordination. Use this when managing complex projects with dependencies, fixed scopes or timelines, multiple stakeholders, or critical success paths.

methodologies.name: clickup-setup
methodologies.description: Extends the agile ClickUp setup for project managers with focus on larger-scale workflows. Covers space-based team isolation, folder-based project grouping, custom fields for cross-team consistency, and dashboard configurations. Includes templates for sprint planning, tracking velocity, and managing multiple concurrent projects.

methodologies.name: github-projects
methodologies.description: Adapts GitHub Projects for project management workflows including cross-repository tracking, custom field standardization, and automation across multiple teams. You learn to create organization-level projects for portfolio views, set up issue templates for consistency, and use GitHub Actions for complex automation. Ideal for managing large engineering initiatives.

methodologies.name: linear-issue-tracking
methodologies.description: Applies Linear to broader project management beyond single teams, including roadmap management, project tracking across teams, and velocity-based capacity planning. You learn to structure multiple teams in one workspace, use projects as strategic initiatives, configure cycles for sync points, and generate insights for portfolio-level reporting.

methodologies.name: notion-pm
methodologies.description: Scales Notion PM setup for managing multiple projects with shared status, resources, and dependencies. You learn to create Teamspaces for different functions, use relations to track projects to tasks to team members, create portfolio views and rollup dashboards, and maintain documentation alongside execution tracking.

methodologies.name: reporting-dashboards
methodologies.description: Teaches how to build dashboards and reports that keep stakeholders informed with minimal meetings. Covers Jira gadgets, Linear insights, automated Slack digests, and email report scheduling. You learn to design reports for different audiences (executive, team, stakeholder), create templates, and maintain dashboard queries that stay current.

methodologies.name: trello-kanban
methodologies.description: Extends Trello beyond simple task boards to project management with sprint planning, portfolio views, and detailed card structures. You learn to template boards for consistency, use card checklists for task breakdown, leverage Power-Ups for advanced features like calendar and voting, and create processes for archiving completed sprints.

domains.description: Product strategy, vision, and roadmap ownership from market discovery through launch and scaling. Covers product discovery, competitive analysis, roadmap planning, metrics definition, feature prioritization, and user feedback. Use this when owning a product end-to-end, making strategic tradeoffs, aligning stakeholders around vision, or steering product direction through market changes.

methodologies.name: backlog-management
methodologies.description: Establishes the principles of a healthy backlog that serves as a strategic tool rather than a dumping ground, following the DEEP model (Detailed, Emergent, Estimated, Prioritized). You learn to capture broadly, triage regularly, refine the top items with acceptance criteria, and clean up ruthlessly. Includes grooming session templates and metrics for backlog health.

methodologies.name: continuous-discovery
methodologies.description: Integrates user research into ongoing product work rather than treating it as separate, allocating 15-20% of team capacity to weekly interviews, data analysis, and experiments. You learn to run discovery alongside development, document learnings in shared repositories, and use insights to inform prioritization. Emphasizes making discovery a habit.

methodologies.name: feature-prioritization-moscow
methodologies.description: Provides the MoSCoW framework for categorizing requirements into Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, and Won't Have buckets tied to fixed constraints. You learn to apply strict tests for each category, allocate effort proportionally (60/20/20 target), and prevent scope creep by explicitly documenting exclusions. Includes stakeholder alignment templates.

methodologies.name: feature-prioritization-rice
methodologies.description: Introduces the quantitative RICE framework (Reach × Impact × Confidence / Effort) for objectively comparing features across projects. You learn to estimate each factor with clear definitions, calculate scores, and rank by priority while sanity-checking the results. Replaces gut-feel prioritization with data-driven decisions.

methodologies.name: micro-mvps
methodologies.description: Teaches rapid validation through extremely small experiments designed to test one assumption at a time, from landing pages to videos to concierge MVPs. Each micro-MVP takes days to hours rather than weeks, allowing you to fail fast before investing in full builds. Includes examples like Dropbox's 3-minute video that validated the core assumption.

methodologies.name: mvp-scoping
methodologies.description: Provides the process for determining the right scope for a minimum viable product that solves a core problem completely while remaining buildable in your timeframe. You learn to define the core value, apply MoSCoW to features, validate scope through key questions, and define success metrics before building. Emphasizes that MVP is about learning, not launching perfection.

methodologies.name: okr-setting
methodologies.description: OKRs solve the problem of vague or rigid goal-setting by combining qualitative, inspiring objectives with quantitative key results. Apply this framework when you need to align your team around measurable progress and prevent goal-setting from becoming either too abstract or too prescriptive. OKRs connect ambition to measurable outcomes while preventing teams from losing focus by setting too many objectives, and they provide a structured way to track progress and conduct meaningful check-ins throughout a quarter.

methodologies.name: outcome-based-roadmaps
methodologies.description: Outcome-based roadmaps solve feature-based roadmaps that commit to solutions before validating problems, replacing feature lists with outcome goals that allow flexibility in solutions. Use this when you want stakeholders aligned on what you're trying to achieve rather than locked into specific features, which is especially valuable in uncertain markets where the path to the goal may need to pivot. The approach delivers better stakeholder conversations because it separates the problem from the solution, allowing teams to experiment with different approaches while remaining committed to the outcome.

methodologies.name: outcome-based-roadmaps-advanced
methodologies.description: Advanced outcome roadmapping handles stakeholder pressure for feature timelines while maintaining outcome focus by using different roadmap styles for different audiences and decomposing business goals into product outcomes with clear success criteria. Apply this when you have multiple stakeholder groups with conflicting communication needs or when you need to translate high-level business goals into actionable product outcomes. This methodology delivers clarity on what outcomes drive revenue while providing executives quarterly metrics and teams with sprint-level guidance, making the organization's impact architecture visible.

methodologies.name: product-discovery
methodologies.description: Product discovery reduces the risk of building features nobody wants by systematically identifying and validating assumptions before committing engineering resources, addressing value, usability, feasibility, and business viability risks. Use this methodology when you have untested ideas or significant uncertainties about whether customers will use or pay for your solution, spending 20-30% of capacity on cheap experiments. The discovery process delivers validated learning that allows you to pivot quickly or proceed with confidence, turning vague ideas into evidence-backed development plans.

methodologies.name: product-launch
methodologies.description: Product launches fail without coordination, but structured launch playbooks solve this by defining positioning, creating assets, building pre-launch audiences, and coordinating across channels. Apply this framework whenever you're introducing a product to market, whether it's a soft launch to early adopters or a full public launch, because even internal releases benefit from strategic communication. A launch playbook delivers momentum and awareness by orchestrating multiple channels with consistent messaging, enabling teams to capture the moment when users are most receptive to learning about what you've built.

methodologies.name: roadmap-design
methodologies.description: Roadmap design addresses the problem of feature lists with dates that always slip by using outcome-focused formats that provide strategic clarity without false certainty, choosing the right roadmap type for your audience. Use roadmap design when you need to communicate direction to stakeholders while preserving flexibility to learn and iterate, because different audiences need different formats: customers want themes, executives want outcomes, and engineers need confidence levels. This methodology delivers strategic alignment by connecting work to objectives while managing stakeholder expectations around timing and scope.

methodologies.name: spec-writing
methodologies.description: Specification writing solves the problem of vague or prescriptive specs that confuse teams by defining WHAT to build and WHY without dictating HOW, using a balanced template that captures problems, goals, requirements, and acceptance criteria. Apply specs when you want to prevent scope creep, ensure designers and engineers share understanding, and leave room for innovation in implementation. Well-written specifications deliver alignment and reduce rework by making success criteria explicit and removing ambiguity about what constitutes completion.

methodologies.name: user-story-mapping
methodologies.description: User story mapping solves the problem of flat backlogs losing context by arranging stories into a visual model that shows the complete user journey horizontally and release priorities vertically. Use this technique when you want teams to understand how individual stories fit into the user's complete workflow, preventing you from shipping disconnected features that don't enable the core job. Story maps deliver scope clarity and better release sequencing by making it obvious what minimum set of tasks completes an end-to-end user journey.

domains.description: Day-to-day product operations including prioritization, backlog management, analytics, and feedback loops. Covers RICE and MoSCoW prioritization, feature backlog grooming, product metrics, user feedback collection, technical debt management, and lifecycle analysis. Use this when maintaining product health, keeping backlog organized, driving data-driven decisions, managing stakeholder feedback, or scaling product processes.

methodologies.name: backlog-management
methodologies.description: Backlog management prevents backlogs from becoming dumping grounds for random ideas by maintaining a prioritized, healthy list where items are detailed at the top and rough at the bottom, following DEEP and INVEST principles. Maintain backlog health through weekly grooming, categorizing items, and regularly cleaning up stale entries, keeping backlogs to 2-4 sprints worth of ready items with less than 10% stale items. A healthy backlog delivers faster iteration because teams always have clear, well-understood work ready to build, and new prioritization data updates priorities continuously.

methodologies.name: feature-prioritization-rice
methodologies.description: RICE prioritization solves gut-feeling feature decisions by using a quantitative scoring framework that weighs Reach, Impact, Confidence, and Effort to objectively compare features across different metrics. Use RICE when you want to deprioritize HiPPO decisions and need a repeatable framework for ranking candidates, because the formula accounts for both upside potential and implementation cost. RICE delivers defensible prioritization because scores are based on data and reasoning, not opinions, and the framework surfaces when effort assumptions are too high or confidence too low.

methodologies.name: feedback-management
methodologies.description: Feedback management solves the problem of feedback scattered across channels and loudest voices dominating by centralizing collection, categorizing by type and topic, analyzing patterns, and closing the loop with users. Establish a single source of truth for feedback, triage and categorize everything, aggregate to find patterns, prioritize using RICE or similar frameworks, and always respond to tell users what happened. A systematic feedback process delivers better product decisions because you discover real patterns in what users need instead of being swayed by individual voices, and users feel heard because their input visibly affects your roadmap.

methodologies.name: product-analytics
methodologies.description: Product analytics solves the problem of collecting data without understanding it by creating a framework that connects metrics to product decisions, starting with defining what you're trying to learn rather than tracking everything. Define key questions first, create a tracking plan with standardized event names, implement tracking server-side for critical events, build audience-specific dashboards, and establish regular analysis rituals. Analytics delivers decision-making capability by showing what users actually do versus what you assume, and cohort and segment analysis reveals who your power users are and what paths drive retention.

methodologies.name: product-lifecycle
methodologies.description: Product lifecycle management solves resource misallocation by recognizing that Introduction, Growth, Maturity, and Decline stages require fundamentally different strategies, metrics, and investments. Assess your product's current stage using growth rate, profitability, competition, and innovation indicators, then apply stage-appropriate strategies rather than using one playbook for all products. This framework delivers efficient resource allocation because you stop applying growth tactics to declining products and stop over-investing in mature products, instead matching investment to the product's actual market opportunity.

methodologies.name: technical-debt-management
methodologies.description: Technical debt management solves the invisible productivity slowdown by making debt visible, quantifying its impact, prioritizing ruthlessly, and allocating consistent capacity to pay it down. Make debt visible by tagging items and tracking them centrally, quantify interest by measuring how much it slows development, prioritize by impact and alignment to planned work, allocate 15-20% capacity continuously, and prevent new debt through Definition of Done. Systematic debt paydown delivers velocity recovery because teams understand the actual cost of shortcuts, and removing high-impact debt unblocks future work.

domains.description: Product planning from ideation through launch covering MVP scoping, roadmaps, discovery, and user story mapping. Covers minimum product frameworks, outcome-based roadmaps, OKR setting, user story mapping, release planning, launch strategy, and competitive positioning. Use this when defining a new product, planning feature releases, discovering user needs, or planning major launches.

methodologies.name: continuous-discovery
methodologies.description: Continuous discovery solves the problem of one-time discovery activities by integrating discovery into the regular sprint cycle with weekly user interviews, daily data review, weekly competitor monitoring, and daily support analysis. Allocate 15-20% of team capacity to ongoing discovery, run experiments alongside development, document learnings centrally, and share insights across teams rather than treating discovery as a separate phase. This approach delivers faster learning cycles because you're constantly validating assumptions against real usage, preventing you from building in isolation.

methodologies.name: micro-mvps
methodologies.description: Micro-MVPs solve the problem of traditional MVPs taking weeks to months by designing extremely small, high-signal experiments that validate one key assumption at a time, from landing pages and concierge services to video demos and fake door tests. Use micro-MVPs when you're uncertain about whether customers want your solution but time-constrained, because each experiment takes days or hours rather than weeks. This methodology delivers rapid learning by running multiple assumption tests in the time a traditional MVP takes to build, letting you fail fast and pivot before committing real engineering resources.

methodologies.name: minimum-product-frameworks
methodologies.description: The 9 minimum product frameworks solve the problem of MVP being outdated advice by providing nine different starting points depending on your market conditions: MVP for unvalidated ideas in blue oceans, MLP for crowded markets, MMP for enterprise, MFP for technical uncertainty. Choose your framework based on whether you're in a blue ocean validating demand or a red ocean competing against existing solutions, because in crowded markets you need to be lovable or awesome to switch users from their current solutions. These frameworks deliver market-appropriate launches because they match ambition level to market conditions, preventing the suicide-by-MVP problem where you ship something barely better than existing solutions.

methodologies.name: mlp-planning
methodologies.description: MLP planning solves the problem of MVP validating ideas but failing to retain users by evolving from viable to lovable through four layers: functional (core value), reliable (consistent experience), usable (easy to learn), and delightful (emotional satisfaction). Audit your MVP against all four layers, identify where features fall short, prioritize polish opportunities by frequency and visibility, and plan specific delight improvements for key moments. This framework delivers retention improvement because you're not adding more features but polishing the experience of existing features, making core interactions faster, more reliable, and emotionally satisfying.

methodologies.name: mvp-scoping
methodologies.description: MVP scoping solves the problem of founders building too much or too little for first versions by defining the smallest version that solves a core problem, delivers value, and allows you to learn. Define your core problem end-to-end, identify the one thing that must work, list all features and categorize them, apply MoSCoW to separate must-haves from nice-to-haves, and validate that you can build it in your target timeframe. This framework delivers focused launches because you deliver complete value in a single area rather than partial value across many areas, and you learn what matters from real users rather than building in isolation.

methodologies.name: okr-setting
methodologies.description: OKRs solve goal-setting that's either too vague or too rigid by combining qualitative, inspiring objectives with quantitative key results, creating focus around what matters most. Set 3-5 objectives quarterly with 3-5 key results each, using strict tests to ensure objectives are inspiring while key results are measurable outcomes not tasks, track weekly with monthly scoring. OKRs deliver alignment and learning because teams understand why their work matters, and the 70% confidence scoring celebrates ambitious failure instead of treating misses as mistakes.

methodologies.name: outcome-based-roadmaps
methodologies.description: Outcome-based roadmaps solve feature-based roadmaps that lock you into solutions by showing what problems you're solving rather than what features you're building, allowing solution flexibility. Present the problem and desired outcome, show data supporting the priority, outline potential solutions without committing to one, and define success criteria upfront. This approach delivers strategic alignment because stakeholders debate the goal, not the feature, and you preserve the flexibility to pivot solutions based on what you learn.

methodologies.name: outcome-based-roadmaps-advanced
methodologies.description: Advanced outcome roadmapping solves stakeholder pressure for commitments while maintaining outcome focus by tailoring roadmap style to audience, decomposing business goals into product outcomes, and using confidence levels instead of commitments. Create different roadmaps for different audiences: customers see themes without dates, executives see quarterly outcome metrics, engineers see sprint-level guidance, and salespeople see problem-solution positioning. This framework delivers transparent impact because the organization can see exactly how product outcomes drive business revenue, and different stakeholders get the format they need.

methodologies.name: product-discovery
methodologies.description: Product discovery reduces the risk of building wrong features by identifying and validating assumptions around value, usability, feasibility, and business viability before committing resources. List all assumptions, prioritize high-risk ones that would kill the idea if wrong, design focused experiments for each, run them quickly, and make explicit decisions about proceeding or pivoting. Discovery delivers de-risked development because you've tested whether customers will use and pay for your solution before engineering builds it.

methodologies.name: product-launch
methodologies.description: Product launches fail without coordination, but structured planning solves this by defining strategy, creating assets, building pre-launch audience, and orchestrating launch day across channels. Start planning 6-8 weeks out with positioning, create assets in parallel with audience building, lock down messaging for each channel, and prepare teams for launch day. A launch playbook delivers momentum by coordinating timing and messaging so your launch reaches people when they're most receptive.

methodologies.name: release-planning
methodologies.description: Release planning structures the decision of what ships when and how it's communicated. It answers what goes in each release, who it's for, and how to ensure readiness through testing, stakeholder briefing, and communication planning. Apply when bundling features for customers to ensure quality, minimize risk, and maintain momentum.

methodologies.name: roadmap-design
methodologies.description: Roadmap design creates a strategic communication tool showing what you plan to build and why, using formats that match your audience's uncertainty level. It transforms vague feature lists into clear outcome-focused plans with themes, confidence levels, and explicit exclusions. Use to align stakeholders, guide development decisions, and communicate product direction.

methodologies.name: user-story-mapping
methodologies.description: User story mapping arranges stories into a visual model showing user activities horizontally and priority vertically, creating a complete picture of user workflows. It identifies the walking skeleton (minimum viable flow), slices releases, and prevents missing critical steps. Apply when prioritizing backlogs to ensure features deliver coherent user value and catch dependencies.

domains.description: Market research and competitive analysis to understand industry dynamics, customer segments, and market opportunities. Covers market sizing, competitive landscaping, trend analysis, customer segmentation, and market opportunity assessment. Use this when evaluating new markets, understanding competitive threats, sizing addressable markets, identifying underserved segments, or validating market assumptions.

methodologies.name: idea-generation
methodologies.description: Idea generation is a systematic process combining your skills, market needs, and interests to discover business opportunities using seven frameworks: skills inventory, pain point mining, job substitution, productized services, unbundling, market stacking, and personal problems. Score ideas on market size, fit, competition, monetization, and speed to MVP to identify viable opportunities.

methodologies.name: idea-generation-methods
methodologies.description: Idea generation methods provides four systematic approaches: the 7 Ps framework (Pain, Passion, Profession, Process, Platform, People, Product), Paul Graham's four questions about tedious tasks and surprising hardness, pain point research through observation and mining, and niche evaluation scoring. Use to generate and validate multiple ideas systematically rather than relying on inspiration.

methodologies.name: naming-and-domains
methodologies.description: Naming and domain methodology covers choosing memorable project names and securing matching domains and social handles. It documents seven naming strategies (descriptive, invented, compound, metaphor, portmanteau, alliteration, acronym) with criteria for good names and a systematic process for generating and validating candidates against availability.
